what_it_does: This orienteering entry documents a single-file .mcp.json launch configuration that registers an MCP server named company-channel; when a Claude Code session starts in /home/tim/channel-test, that server runs node /home/tim/company/channels/company_channel.mjs with environment variables COMPANY_CHANNEL_PORT=8795, COMPANY_CHANNEL_HANDLE=tim-live-1, and COMPANY_ROOT=/home/tim/company, joining the session to the Company cross-session channel fabric.
observations: The described config is a single 303-byte .mcp.json file.
It registers one MCP server called company-channel.
Environment variables hardcode port 8795, handle tim-live-1, and company root /home/tim/company.
The entry marks status as unconfirmed and kind as config.
It is classified as external to the company root and has no git remote.
secrets is false and move_intent is none.
Coverage is 1 of 1 file read, last read 2026-06-26.
standouts: It is an external launcher for an internal Company channel transport.
The handle tim-live-1 and port 8795 are the mesh membership coordinates.
It is explicitly framed as a test/bootstrap harness rather than production infrastructure.
conventions: Follows the terrain-entry frontmatter schema with type, name, register, aliases, path, relation, kind, status, dates, coverage, and tags.
Uses the descriptive register for non-authoritative documentation.
Marks relation: external because the config lives outside /home/tim/company.
Includes an inline evidence block quoting the full .mcp.json content.
concerns: Absolute paths and handle/port are hardcoded in the config.
status: unconfirmed means the described setup has not been verified.
No git remote or provenance for the config file.
The entry did not trace company_channel.mjs itself, so behavior depends on unexamined code.
notes: A minimal Claude Code MCP bootstrap config that brings a named session onto the Company channel mesh.
questions: Is the .mcp.json file actually present at /home/tim/channel-test?
How does company_channel.mjs handle authentication or multiple handles on the same port?
Why is the entry status unconfirmed if the config was read in full?
Is port 8795 reserved or could it conflict with other mesh members?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the entry states it documents a single-file launch config for joining the fabric, and the body describes that config, its command, env vars, and mesh role.
apparent_themes: session identity via handle and port
intention_signals: It is trying to create a portable, drop-in way to enroll any Claude Code working directory as a named peer in the Company channel mesh.
novelty: It treats a local .mcp.json as a fabric membership ticket and records the launcher as a terrain node rather than as source code.
contribution: It preserves the exact launch command, environment variables, handle, port, and root path needed to instantiate a mesh member, and it marks the boundary between the external config at /home/tim/channel-test and the Company channel transport implementation.
summary_for_embedding: An orienteering terrain entry for /home/tim/channel-test describing a single-file .mcp.json that launches the Company channel MCP server to join a Claude Code session to the cross-session fabric on port 8795 as handle tim-live-1.
intention_for_embedding: To document the smallest possible bootstrap configuration for bringing a named Claude Code session onto the Company cross-session channel mesh.
